_meta.description: Printer-to-material compatibility matrix. Maps which materials each printer can handle out-of-box, with upgrades, or not at all.

pla_plus.notes: Prints like standard PLA with improved toughness.

silk_pla.notes: Prints like PLA. Slightly higher temp for best sheen.

wood_pla.notes: Prints like PLA. Wood particles are mildly abrasive over time — nozzle wears faster than plain PLA.

petg.notes: Prints well on stock hardware. Reduce fan speed vs PLA for better layer adhesion.

petg_cf.notes: CF-PETG: carbon fiber rapidly wears brass nozzles.

cf_petg.notes: CF-PETG: carbon fiber rapidly wears brass nozzles.

abs.notes: ABS: open frame causes warping and layer splitting.

asa.notes: ASA: open frame causes warping similar to ABS.

asa_plus.notes: ASA+: enclosure needed to prevent warping.

tpu.notes: Direct drive handles TPU well. Print slowly (20-30mm/s) with minimal retraction.

hips.notes: HIPS: warps without enclosure like ABS.

pva.notes: PVA is extremely hygroscopic — absorbs moisture within hours. Dry box storage and feeding mandatory.

nylon.notes: Nylon: severe warping without enclosure, extremely hygroscopic — dry box mandatory.

cf_nylon.notes: CF-Nylon: carbon fiber destroys brass nozzles within hours, warps severely without enclosure, nylon base is extremely hygroscopic — dry box mandatory.

cf_pla.notes: Carbon fiber rapidly wears brass nozzles. Hardened steel or ruby nozzle required.

polycarbonate.notes: Polycarbonate: extreme warping without heated enclosure, very hygroscopic — dry box required.

pc_abs.notes: PC-ABS: warps without enclosure, hygroscopic blend — dry box recommended.

pet_cf.notes: PET-CF: carbon fiber rapidly wears brass nozzles.

pa6_gf.notes: PA6-GF: glass fiber is extremely abrasive — hardened nozzle required, nylon base warps severely without enclosure, nylon base is extremely hygroscopic — dry box mandatory.

abs.notes: Enclosed chamber prevents warping. Good all-around engineering material.

asa.notes: UV-resistant alternative to ABS. Enclosed chamber prevents warping.

asa_plus.notes: Enhanced ASA with better layer adhesion. Same print conditions as standard ASA.

tpu.notes: Direct drive handles TPU well. Print slowly (20-30mm/s) with minimal retraction.

hips.notes: Dissolves in limonene for support removal. Prints like ABS — enclosed chamber recommended.

pva.notes: PVA is extremely hygroscopic — absorbs moisture within hours. Dry box storage and feeding mandatory.

nylon.notes: Nylon: extremely hygroscopic — dry box mandatory.

cf_nylon.notes: CF-Nylon: carbon fiber destroys brass nozzles within hours, nylon base is extremely hygroscopic — dry box mandatory.

cf_pla.notes: Carbon fiber rapidly wears brass nozzles. Hardened steel or ruby nozzle required.

polycarbonate.notes: Polycarbonate: very hygroscopic — dry box required.

pc_abs.notes: PC-ABS: hygroscopic blend — dry box recommended.

pet_cf.notes: PET-CF: carbon fiber rapidly wears brass nozzles.

pa6_gf.notes: PA6-GF: glass fiber is extremely abrasive — hardened nozzle required, nylon base is extremely hygroscopic — dry box mandatory.

pla_plus.notes: Prints like standard PLA with improved toughness.

silk_pla.notes: Prints like PLA. Slightly higher temp for best sheen.

wood_pla.notes: Prints like PLA. Wood particles are mildly abrasive over time — nozzle wears faster than plain PLA.

petg.notes: Prints well on stock hardware. Reduce fan speed vs PLA for better layer adhesion.

petg_cf.notes: Hardened nozzle handles CF abrasion. Stiffer than plain PETG with good layer adhesion.

cf_petg.notes: Hardened nozzle handles CF abrasion. Stiffer than plain PETG with good layer adhesion.

abs.notes: Enclosed chamber prevents warping. Good all-around engineering material.

asa.notes: UV-resistant alternative to ABS. Enclosed chamber prevents warping.

asa_plus.notes: Enhanced ASA with better layer adhesion. Same print conditions as standard ASA.

tpu.notes: Direct drive handles TPU well. Print slowly (20-30mm/s) with minimal retraction.

hips.notes: Dissolves in limonene for support removal. Prints like ABS — enclosed chamber recommended.

pva.notes: PVA is extremely hygroscopic — absorbs moisture within hours. Dry box storage and feeding mandatory.

nylon.notes: Nylon: extremely hygroscopic — dry box mandatory.

cf_nylon.notes: CF-Nylon: nylon base is extremely hygroscopic — dry box mandatory.

cf_pla.notes: Hardened nozzle included. Prints like PLA with much higher stiffness.

polycarbonate.notes: Polycarbonate: very hygroscopic — dry box required.

pc_abs.notes: PC-ABS: hygroscopic blend — dry box recommended.

pet_cf.notes: Hardened nozzle handles CF abrasion. High-stiffness engineering material with good layer adhesion.

pa6_gf.notes: PA6-GF: nylon base is extremely hygroscopic — dry box mandatory.

pla_plus.notes: Prints like standard PLA with improved toughness.

silk_pla.notes: Prints like PLA. Slightly higher temp for best sheen.

wood_pla.notes: Prints like PLA. Wood particles are mildly abrasive over time — nozzle wears faster than plain PLA.

petg.notes: Print at lower end of temp range (230-235C) to stay within PTFE limits. Reduce fan speed vs PLA.

petg_cf.notes: CF-PETG: carbon fiber rapidly wears brass nozzles, print temps exceed PTFE limits.

cf_petg.notes: CF-PETG: carbon fiber rapidly wears brass nozzles, print temps exceed PTFE limits.

abs.notes: ABS: PTFE degrades at ABS temps (240C+), open frame causes warping and layer splitting.

asa.notes: ASA: PTFE degrades at ASA temps (240-260C), open frame causes warping similar to ABS.

asa_plus.notes: ASA+: PTFE hotend not rated for ASA+ temps, enclosure needed to prevent warping.

tpu.notes: TPU buckles in bowden tubes — direct drive extruder required for reliable feeding.

hips.notes: HIPS: PTFE hotend not rated for HIPS temps (230-250C), warps without enclosure like ABS.

pva.notes: PVA: bowden tube causes jams with soft PVA. Extremely hygroscopic — must be stored and fed from dry box.

nylon.notes: Nylon: PTFE cannot handle nylon temps (250-270C), severe warping without enclosure, extremely hygroscopic — dry box mandatory.

cf_nylon.notes: CF-Nylon: carbon fiber destroys brass nozzles within hours, PTFE cannot handle CF-nylon temps (260-280C), warps severely without enclosure, nylon base is extremely hygroscopic — dry box mandatory.

cf_pla.notes: Carbon fiber rapidly wears brass nozzles. Hardened steel or ruby nozzle required.

polycarbonate.notes: Polycarbonate: PC prints at 260-300C — far above PTFE limits, extreme warping without heated enclosure, very hygroscopic — dry box required.

pc_abs.notes: PC-ABS: blend prints at 250-270C — exceeds PTFE limits, warps without enclosure, hygroscopic blend — dry box recommended.

pet_cf.notes: PET-CF: carbon fiber rapidly wears brass nozzles, print temps (250-270C) exceed PTFE limits.

pa6_gf.notes: PA6-GF: glass fiber is extremely abrasive — hardened nozzle required, print temps (260-290C) exceed PTFE limits, nylon base warps severely without enclosure, nylon base is extremely hygroscopic — dry box mandatory.

polycarbonate.notes: Polycarbonate: PC prints at 260-300C — far above PTFE limits, extreme warping without heated enclosure, very hygroscopic — dry box required.

pc_abs.notes: PC-ABS: blend prints at 250-270C — exceeds PTFE limits, warps without enclosure, hygroscopic blend — dry box recommended.

pet_cf.notes: PET-CF: carbon fiber rapidly wears brass nozzles, print temps (250-270C) exceed PTFE limits.

pa6_gf.notes: PA6-GF: glass fiber is extremely abrasive — hardened nozzle required, print temps (260-290C) exceed PTFE limits, nylon base warps severely without enclosure, nylon base is extremely hygroscopic — dry box mandatory.

pla_plus.notes: Prints like standard PLA with improved toughness.

silk_pla.notes: Prints like PLA. Slightly higher temp for best sheen.

wood_pla.notes: Prints like PLA. Wood particles are mildly abrasive over time — nozzle wears faster than plain PLA.

petg.notes: Print at lower end of temp range (230-235C) to stay within PTFE limits. Reduce fan speed vs PLA.

petg_cf.notes: CF-PETG: carbon fiber rapidly wears brass nozzles, print temps exceed PTFE limits.

cf_petg.notes: CF-PETG: carbon fiber rapidly wears brass nozzles, print temps exceed PTFE limits.

abs.notes: ABS: PTFE degrades at ABS temps (240C+).

asa.notes: ASA: PTFE degrades at ASA temps (240-260C).

asa_plus.notes: ASA+: PTFE hotend not rated for ASA+ temps.

tpu.notes: Direct drive handles TPU well. Print slowly (20-30mm/s) with minimal retraction.

hips.notes: HIPS: PTFE hotend not rated for HIPS temps (230-250C).

pva.notes: PVA is extremely hygroscopic — absorbs moisture within hours. Dry box storage and feeding mandatory.

asa.notes: ASA: PTFE degrades at ASA temps (240-260C), open frame causes warping similar to ABS.

tpu.notes: TPU buckles in bowden tubes — direct drive extruder required for reliable feeding.

pva.notes: PVA: bowden tube causes jams with soft PVA. Extremely hygroscopic — must be stored and fed from dry box.
